statement.en: Real-time tuning of approximated explicit MPC of a heat exchanger
statement.summary: Tunable approximated explicit model predictive control (MPC) has the significant benefit of real-time tunability without the necessity of solving the optimization problem online. The concept of tunable explicit MPC requires storing two boundary explicit MPCs having the same setup except for one of the penalty matrices. In the online phase, both controllers are used to evaluate the optimal control action. Based on both optimal control actions, the value of the tuned control action is calculated by linear interpolation according to the current tuning parameter value. This work provides a technique for scaling the tuning parameter, which depends on the current reference value. The tunable explicit MPC was implemented on a laboratory heat exchanger with nonlinear and asymmetric behavior. The s

proposer: Lenka Galčíková, Petronela Belková, Juraj Oravec

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (dated 2026-04-25, future or pre-publication) presenting a novel control technique (tunable explicit MPC with reference-dependent scaling) applied to a real system. The core method is theoretically sound but validation is incomplete (abstract cuts off mid-sentence). The claim is provisionally credible pending full peer review and reproducibility verification.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 参照値が支配する重み付けで、二つの予見的な魂が流体のように混ざり合う。
